title: Tulsi Chalisa
intro: Shri Tulsi Chalisa - Hymn to Goddess Tulsi
description:
॥ Shri Tulsi Chalisa (English Transliteration) ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Shri Tulsi Maharani, karun vinay sir-naya.
Jo mam ho sankat vikat, deejai maat nashaya॥

॥ Chaupai ॥
Namo namo Tulsi Maharani, mahima amit na jaay bakhani.
Diyo Vishnu tumko sanmana, jag mein chhayo suyash mahana.
Vishnupriya jai jayati bhavani, tihoon lok ki ho sukhkhani.
Bhagwat puja kar jo koi, bina tumhare safal na hoi॥
Jin ghar tav nahin hoy nivasa, us par karhin Vishnu nahin basa.
Kare sada jo tav nit sumiran, tehike kaaj hoy sab pooran॥
Kaatik maas mahatma tumhara, tako jaanat sab sansara.
Tav pujan jo karain kunwari, pavai sundar var sukumari॥
Kar jo pujan nitprati nari, sukh sampatti se hoy sukhari.
Vriddha nari karai jo pujan, mile bhakti hovai pulkit man॥
Shraddha se pujai jo koi, bhavnidhi se tar jaavai soi.
Katha Bhagwat yagya karavai, tum bin nahin safalta pavai॥
Chhayo tav pratap jagbhari, dhyavat tumhin sakal chitdhari.
Tumhin maat yantran tantran, sakal kaaj sidhi hovai kshan mein॥
Aushadhi roop aap ho mata, sab jag mein tav yash vikhyata,
Dev rishi muni au tapdhari, karat sada tav jai jaykari॥
Ved puranan tav yash gaya, mahima agam paar nahin paya.
Namo namo jai jai sukhkarani, namo namo jai dukh-nivarani॥
Namo namo sukhsampatti deni, namo namo agh kaatan chheni.
Namo namo bhaktan dukh harni, namo namo dushtan mad chheni॥
Namo namo bhav paar utarani, namo namo parlok sudharani.
Namo namo nij bhakt ubarani, namo namo jankaj sanwarani॥
Namo namo jai kumati nashavani, namo namo sukh upajavani.
Jayati jayati jai Tulsimai, dhyau tumko sheesh navai॥
Nijjan jaani mohi apnao, bigade kaaraj aap banao.
Karun vinay main maat tumhari, pooran aasha karahu hamari॥
Sharan charan kar jori manau, nishdin tere hi gun gaau.
Krahu maat yah ab mopar daya, nirmal hoy sakal mamkaya॥
Mangu maat yah bar deejai, sakal manorath pooran keejai.
Janun nahin kuch nem achara, chhamahu maat apradh hamara॥
Barah maas karai jo puja, ta sam jag mein aur na dooja.
Prathamhi gangajal mangvave, phir sundar snan karave॥
Chandan akshat pushp chadhave, dhoop deep naivedya lagave.
Kare achaman ganga jal se, dhyan kare hridaya nirmal se॥
Paath kare phir chalisa ki, astuti kare maat tulsa ki.
Yah vidhi puja kare hamesha, taake tan nahin rahai klesha॥
Karai maas Kartik ka sadhan, sove nit pavitra sidh hui jaahin. 
Hai yah katha maha sukhdai, padhe sune so bhav tar jai॥
Tulsi maiya tum kalyani, tumhari mahima sab jag jaani. 
Bhaav na tujhe maa nit nit dhyave, gaa gaakar maa tujhe rijhave॥

॥ Doha ॥
Yah ShriTulsi Chalisa paath kare jo koy.
Govind so phal pavahi jo man ichha hoy॥
meaning:
## ॥ Shri Tulsi Chalisa (English Transliteration & Meaning) ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Shri Tulsi Maharani, karun vinay sir-naya.
(O Mother Tulsi Queen, I bow my head in prayer.)
Jo mam ho sankat vikat, deejai maat nashaya॥
(If I have any difficult crisis, O Mother, destroy it.)

॥ Chaupai ॥
Namo namo Tulsi Maharani, mahima amit na jaay bakhani.
(Salutations to Mother Tulsi, your glory is immense and cannot be described.)
Diyo Vishnu tumko sanmana, jag mein chhayo suyash mahana॥
(Lord Vishnu honored you, and your great fame has spread throughout the world.)
Vishnupriya jai jayati bhavani, tihoon lok ki ho sukhkhani.
(Beloved of Vishnu, victory to you, O Goddess; you are the source of happiness in the three worlds.)
Bhagwat puja kar jo koi, bina tumhare safal na hoi॥
(Whoever performs worship of the Lord, without you, it is not successful.)
Jin ghar tav nahin hoy nivasa, us par karhin Vishnu nahin basa.
(In the home where you do not reside, Lord Vishnu also does not dwell.)
Kare sada jo tav nit sumiran, tehike kaaj hoy sab pooran॥
(Whoever always remembers you daily, all their tasks are fulfilled.)
Kaatik maas mahatma tumhara, tako jaanat sab sansara.
(The whole world knows your greatness in the month of Kartik.)
Tav pujan jo karain kunwari, pavai sundar var sukumari॥
(The maiden who worships you attains a beautiful husband.)
Kar jo pujan nitprati nari, sukh sampatti se hoy sukhari.
(The woman who worships you daily becomes happy with wealth and prosperity.)
Vriddha nari karai jo pujan, mile bhakti hovai pulkit man॥
(The elderly woman who performs your worship attains devotion and a joyful heart.)
Shraddha se pujai jo koi, bhavnidhi se tar jaavai soi.
(Whoever worships you with faith, crosses the ocean of worldly existence.)
Katha Bhagwat yagya karavai, tum bin nahin safalta pavai॥
(Whoever organizes Bhagwat Katha or Yagna, without you, they do not attain success.)
Chhayo tav pratap jagbhari, dhyavat tumhin sakal chitdhari.
(Your glory covers the entire world; everyone meditates upon you with focused minds.)
Tumhin maat yantran tantran, sakal kaaj sidhi hovai kshan mein॥
(O Mother, you are in all Yantras and Tantras; all tasks are accomplished in an instant through you.)
Aushadhi roop aap ho mata, sab jag mein tav yash vikhyata,
(O Mother, you are in the form of medicine; your fame is famous throughout the world.)
Dev rishi muni au tapdhari, karat sada tav jai jaykari॥
(Gods, sages, munis, and ascetics always praise and hail you.)
Ved puranan tav yash gaya, mahima agam paar nahin paya.
(The Vedas and Puranas have sung your praise; no one has found the limit of your infinite glory.)
Namo namo jai jai sukhkarani, namo namo jai dukh-nivarani॥
(Victory to you, the giver of happiness; victory to you, the remover of sorrows.)
Namo namo sukhsampatti deni, namo namo agh kaatan chheni॥
(Salutations to the giver of happiness and wealth; salutations to the one who cuts through sins like a chisel.)
Namo namo bhaktan dukh harni, namo namo dushtan mad chheni॥
(Salutations to the remover of devotees' sorrows; salutations to the destroyer of the wicked's ego.)
Namo namo bhav paar utarani, namo namo parlok sudharani.
(Salutations to the one who helps us cross the worldly ocean; salutations to the one who improves our afterlife.)
Namo namo nij bhakt ubarani, namo namo jankaj sanwarani॥
(Salutations to the savior of your own devotees; salutations to the one who arranges the tasks of the people.)
Namo namo jai kumati nashavani, namo namo sukh upajavani.
(Salutations to the destroyer of bad intellect; salutations to the creator of happiness.)
Jayati jayati jai Tulsimai, dhyau tumko sheesh navai॥
(Victory, victory to you, O Mother Tulsi; I meditate upon you with my head bowed.)
Nijjan jaani mohi apnao, bigade kaaraj aap banao.
(Consider me your own and accept me; fix my ruined tasks yourself.)
Karun vinay main maat tumhari, pooran aasha karahu hamari॥
(O Mother, I pray to you; please fulfill my hopes.)
Sharan charan kar jori manau, nishdin tere hi gun gaau.
(I pray to you with folded hands at your feet; I will sing your praises day and night.)
Krahu maat yah ab mopar daya, nirmal hoy sakal mamkaya॥
(O Mother, have mercy on me now so that my entire body and soul become pure.)
Mangu maat yah bar deejai, sakal manorath pooran keejai.
(O Mother, grant me this boon; fulfill all my desires.)
Janun nahin kuch nem achara, chhamahu maat apradh hamara॥
(I do not know any rules or rituals; O Mother, please forgive my offenses.)
Barah maas karai jo puja, ta sam jag mein aur na dooja.
(Whoever worships you for twelve months, there is no one else like them in the world.)
Prathamhi gangajal mangvave, phir sundar snan karave॥
(First, get Ganges water, then give a beautiful bath.)
Chandan akshat pushp chadhave, dhoop deep naivedya lagave.
(Offer sandalwood, rice grains, and flowers; offer incense, lamps, and food.)
Kare achaman ganga jal se, dhyan kare hridaya nirmal se॥
(Perform Achaman with Ganges water and meditate with a pure heart.)
Paath kare phir chalisa ki, astuti kare maat tulsa ki.
(Then recite the Chalisa and praise Mother Tulsi.)
Yah vidhi puja kare hamesha, taake tan nahin rahai klesha॥
(Whoever worships in this manner always, will have no suffering in their body.)
Karai maas Kartik ka sadhan, sove nit pavitra sidh hui jaahin.
(Whoever practices Sadhana in the month of Kartik, becomes pure and attains perfection.)
Hai yah katha maha sukhdai, padhe sune so bhav tar jai॥
(This story is very blissful; whoever reads or hears it crosses the worldly ocean.)
Tulsi maiya tum kalyani, tumhari mahima sab jag jaani.
(O Mother Tulsi, you are auspicious; your glory is known to all the world.)
Bhaav na tujhe maa nit nit dhyave, gaa gaakar maa tujhe rijhave॥
(Whoever meditates on you daily with devotion and sings to please you, O Mother...)

॥ Doha ॥
Yah ShriTulsi Chalisa paath kare jo koy.
(Whoever recites this Shri Tulsi Chalisa,)
Govind so phal pavahi jo man ichha hoy॥
(Attains the fruits by the grace of Govind, whatever their heart desires.)

faq.q1: What is the main benefit of reading the Tulsi Chalisa at home?
faq.a1: Reciting the Tulsi Chalisa removes negative energy from the house and invites happiness, peace, and prosperity into your home.
faq.q2: Is it necessary to sit near the Tulsi plant to read it?
faq.a2: Reading it while sitting near the Tulsi plant is considered best, but if that is not possible, you can also recite it in your home temple.
faq.q3: What is the best time to read the Tulsi Chalisa?
faq.a3: It provides the best results when recited in the morning, immediately after offering water to the Tulsi plant.
faq.q4: Is it forbidden to read the Tulsi Chalisa on Sundays?
faq.a4: Traditionally, watering or touching the Tulsi plant is avoided on Sundays, but you can still recite the Tulsi Chalisa on that day.
faq.q5: Does it help in attaining Lord Vishnu’s grace?
faq.a5: Yes, Maa Tulsi is very dear to Lord Vishnu. Reciting her Chalisa helps the devotee automatically receive Lord Vishnu’s blessings and grace.
